evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix with dual colormap (viridis diagonal, Blues off-diagonal), all cells annotated, axes labeled True/Predicted class — brief fully satisfied.
evidence.typography: PDF uses Type3 DejaVuSans fonts (not TrueType/Type42); pdf.fonttype=42 not set in script; default DejaVu Sans used throughout instead of a publication font.
evidence.palette: Blues (sequential) for off-diagonal misclassifications and viridis (perceptually uniform) for diagonal — both colorblind-safe; no red/green directional misuse.
evidence.layout_density: 7x6 inch canvas for 10x10 grid is adequate; tight_layout applied; but only one colorbar shown (off-diagonal), leaving diagonal viridis scale unexplained visually.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure and includes specific accuracy range (69%–92%); however, absence of a second colorbar for the diagonal viridis scale would likely draw a reviewer comment.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF embeds heatmap body as a 749x749 raster XObject image and uses Type3 (bitmap) fonts — text is not selectable; pdf.fonttype=42 absent from script.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (26 KB), figure.png (62 KB), figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains row=true/col=predicted convention, dual colormap rationale, adaptive text color logic, and quantifies per-class accuracy range (69% C1 to 92% C8).

overall_strengths: Dual-colormap design cleanly separates diagonal (correct) from off-diagonal (error) cells, making the confusion structure immediately legible.
Caption is publication-quality: it explains the encoding, the color logic, and provides a concrete numerical finding.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 not set, so the PDF contains Type3 bitmap fonts and a raster heatmap image — text is not selectable and the file is not truly vector.
No colorbar for the diagonal viridis scale; readers cannot decode the absolute accuracy values from the diagonal shading without it.
